insight: Properties with higher overall quality ratings tend to have significantly higher sale prices.
Newer properties generally command higher prices due to modern construction and lower maintenance costs.
Neighborhood quality is one of the strongest factors affecting property value.
Larger living area (square footage) directly increases property price.
Properties with garages and parking space tend to have higher value.
Presence of central air conditioning increases buyer demand and property price.
Higher number of bathrooms and bedrooms increases property attractiveness and price.
Renovated homes (recent remodeling) often sell at higher prices.
Properties with finished basements tend to have higher overall value.
Poor exterior condition can significantly reduce property value.
Properties in areas with good infrastructure and connectivity have higher appreciation potential.
High overall condition rating indicates better maintenance and increases property price.
Corner plots may have higher visibility but not always higher value compared to standard plots.
Properties with modern kitchens and higher kitchen quality ratings sell at premium prices.
Fireplaces and luxury amenities can increase perceived property value.
Older properties may be undervalued but can offer renovation-based investment opportunities.
High demand areas tend to show faster price growth compared to average locations.
Properties with larger lot areas are generally more valuable.
Energy-efficient homes are becoming more desirable and may command higher prices.
Properties with poor functional layout may have lower resale value.
